Let me reconsider the problem completely. I've been clicking on:

- id=15: pixels=3, boundary=[[27, 9], [27, 11]] - small horizontal bar above yellow seed area
- id=21: pixels=5, boundary=[[41, 22], [41, 26]] - horizontal bar (part of container?)
- id=29: pixels=45, boundary=[[63, 0], [63, 44]] - bottom bar (timer/HUD)

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

# List all non-HUD objects (exclude the bottom bar and text)
print("All objects on board:")
for n in nodes:
    # Skip the bottom timer bar (id=29, very wide at bottom)
    if n['boundary'][0][0] >= 60:
        continue
    # Skip text (STEP and number)
    if n['pixels'] > 20 and n['color'] in ['R', 'w', 'W']:
        continue
    print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}, children={n['children']}")

# Also check adjacency
print("\nAdjacency list:")
for pair in seg['adjacency_list']:
    print(f"  {pair}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

# Find container frames (g color, large objects)
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'g' and n['pixels'] == 36:
        print(f"Container: id={n['id']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
        # Get center
        center_row = sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        center_col = sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        print(f"  Center: ({center_row}, {center_col})")

# Try clicking on the top container frame (id=9)
# boundary=[[18, 36], [18, 48], [24, 48], [24, 36]]
# Center is around (21, 42)
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 21, 'col': 42}])
print(f"\nAfter clicking top container: game_over={last_action_result.get('game_over')}, done={last_action_result.get('done')}, level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")

# Check if anything changed
seg2 = current_frame.segmentation
print("Objects after click:")
for n in seg2['nodes']:
    if n['boundary'][0][0] >= 60 or n['pixels'] > 20 and n['color'] in ['R', 'w', 'W']:
        continue
    if n['color'] in ['g', 'c', 'N', 'Y', 'G', 'r', 'B']:
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
